# TARA — Claude Code Project Memory
# Training Analytics & Resource Agent
# AI Chief of Training — Universal L&D Platform
# Version 3.0 — May 2026
# Authors: Tony Thomas + Dharam
# Do not edit this file during a build session.
# Update only when a confirmed product decision changes.

---

## WHAT TARA IS

TARA is a Mini LMS Analytics Platform.
It sits alongside any LMS and does what
no LMS has ever done —
connect training activity to commercial outcomes.

The LMS answers: Did they do it?
TARA answers:    Did it work?

TARA is not an LMS.
TARA does not deliver training content.
TARA does not replace the LMS.
TARA reads data and produces commercial intelligence.

---

## THE ONE PROBLEM TARA SOLVES

Training data sits in silos.
Performance data sits somewhere else.
Connecting them manually takes hours or days.
The output is still just numbers with no story.
Organisations end up fixing the wrong problem
because finding the real one takes too long.

TARA uploads three files.
60 seconds later the training manager
has the commercial answer —
in their currency, from their data,
ready for their next meeting.

---

## THE ONE SENTENCE

"Tell TARA what success looks like.
Upload your data.
TARA tells you whether your training
is delivering it — and exactly what
to fix if it is not."

---

## THE CORE PRODUCT PHILOSOPHY

### 1. The organisation defines success
Every metric is user-defined at setup.
TARA never assumes what success looks like.

Examples:
Retail:      Revenue per employee (AED / USD / GBP)
Healthcare:  Patient satisfaction score
Banking:     Policies sold per adviser
Logistics:   Deliveries per driver per day
Technology:  Customer retention rate / ARR per CSM

### 2. TARA speaks their language
Every output uses their metric,
their currency, their terminology.
Never L&D jargon to a business leader.
Completions and pass rates are never the output.
Revenue, productivity, and retention are the output.

### 3. Preparation not interruption
TARA is used in the 24-48 hours before a meeting.
Not during it.
A prepared manager is more credible
than one looking things up at the table.
The training manager who always has
the commercial answer — that is the identity
TARA creates.

### 4. Fix the right thing first
Most organisations fix the wrong problem
because finding the real one takes too long.
TARA finds the real problem in 60 seconds.
It also tells the manager which gaps are
costing the most commercially —
so effort goes where it matters.

### 5. Works for team meetings not just leadership
TARA is not only for board presentations.
It is for weekly team meetings.
For sharing the right data with direct reports.
For making the next decision based on evidence.
For knowing where the gaps are before
spending time in the wrong area.

---

## WHO TARA SERVES

Three users in every organisation:

### USER 1 — The Training Manager (primary buyer)
Needs:
- What is the real problem?
- Where is the gap?
- What do I fix first?
- Am I ready for my meeting?
- Which gaps are costing us the most?

### USER 2 — The Team Leader / Store Manager
Needs:
- Which of my people need help?
- What exactly do I say to them?
- What does good look like?

### USER 3 — The Regional Director / CLO
Needs:
- Is training working commercially?
- What is the return on investment?
- Where should we focus budget?

---

## THE VIRAL LOOP

TARA spreads inside organisations upward.
This is B2B viral — more defensible than B2C.

Training manager uploads data
         ↓
TARA produces commercial insight in 60 seconds
         ↓
Manager walks into leadership meeting prepared
         ↓
Director asks "how did you get this?"
         ↓
Regional head wants TARA for their markets
         ↓
CLO wants TARA across the group

One CSV upload. One meeting.
TARA spreads without advertising.

## THE FROZEN CATEGORY FRAMEWORK

TARA classifies every in-scope employee
monthly using two dimensions:

Dimension 1: Training Engagement
Requires BOTH frequency (sessions completed)
AND quality (knowledge score vs threshold).
Default threshold: 15 sessions AND 80% score.
Configurable per organisation at setup.

Dimension 2: Commercial Performance
Measured against the organisation's
primary commercial KPI.
Benchmarked within peer group.
Configurable method: percentile, vs target,
vs peer, or grade-based thresholds.

### Five categories — universal across industries:

⭐ STAR
High training + High performance
Intervention: Stretch content, recognition,
succession pathway flag

📗 Learner
High training + Low performance
Root cause: APPLICATION gap — not knowledge gap
Intervention: Check behavioural nodes FIRST
COACH floor observation brief — not more content

💪 Performer
Low training + High performance
Root cause: COMPLIANCE gap — instinctive skills
Intervention: Targeted LP framed as
professional development not enforcement

🔴 Needs Support
Low training + Low performance
Root cause: FOUNDATION gap
Intervention: 30-day recovery plan,
daily manager check-in

⚫ Missing Data
No training record at all
Root cause: Enrolment or onboarding issue
Intervention: Escalate to L&D team

### THE LEARNER PARADOX — non-negotiable:
High knowledge score + low performance
= BEHAVIOURAL gap.
Intervention: Manager observation on the floor.
NOT more training content. Ever.
ATLAS always checks for this before
generating any intervention.
COACH always generates an observation guide
not a content brief when this is detected.

### Excluded roles:
Senior leadership always excluded from
frontline categorisation unless explicitly
included by the L&D manager at setup.
Configurable per workspace.

---

## THE THREE FILES TARA READS

Zero live connections to any system.
Manual uploads only.
All data stays on the client's machine.
Nothing sent to any cloud except
message text to Anthropic API for processing.

### FILE 1 — COMMERCIAL PERFORMANCE DATA
What it is:
Sales or commercial KPI performance
per employee per period.

What it contains:
Employee ID, Name, Role,
Location / Market / Store,
Primary KPI value for the period.

Examples of primary KPI:
Net sales per SA (retail)
Revenue per adviser (finance)
Policies sold per agent (insurance)
Patient satisfaction score (healthcare)
Deliveries per driver (logistics)

Frequency: Monthly upload
Source: Sales system, HRIS, POS export

### FILE 2 — LMS DATA
What it is:
Training engagement data from any LMS.

Format options — one or two files
depending on what the LMS exports:

Option A — One combined file:
Employee ID, Sessions count,
Knowledge score, Topic name,
Pass/fail status, Assessment date.
TARA detects both session and score data
from the single file automatically.

Option B — Two separate files:
File 2A — Training Sessions Export:
  Employee ID, Total sessions completed,
  Daily session frequency, Team hierarchy.
File 2B — Knowledge Score Export:
  Employee ID, Topic name,
  Assessment score, Pass/fail status.
TARA merges on Employee ID automatically.

The user selects one file or two at upload.
Column mapping confirmed once.
Saved permanently for future uploads.

Frequency: Monthly upload
Source: Any LMS — Axonify, Cornerstone,
Workday Learning, SAP SuccessFactors,
Moodle, TalentLMS, or any platform
that exports CSV or XLSX.

### FILE 3 — EMPLOYEE DATABASE
What it is:
The master staff roster.

What it contains:
Employee ID, Full name, Job title,
Role code, Team, Market / Location,
Manager name, Active / Inactive status,
Hire date (optional).

Frequency: Monthly or on any roster change
Source: HR system or LMS user export

---

## DATA RULES — NON-NEGOTIABLE

### Employee ID normalisation
Every file uses a different format.
Mixed float and string formats are common.
Always normalise across all three files.
Pattern: str(int(float(emp_id)))
This is the most common cause of
silent merge failures. Always fix this first.

### Strip trailing spaces from column headers
Common in Axonify and SAP exports.
"Emp Code " (with trailing space) ≠ "Emp Code"
Strip all column headers before any operation.

### Filter structural zeros
Score = 0 AND status = passed
= data artefact not a real assessment result.
This is a known Axonify Footwear Anatomy
certification issue.
Always filter before calculating averages.
Filter: NOT (score == 0 AND status == 'passed')

### Behavioural node scores
SM observation rubric ONLY.
Never derive behavioural scores from LMS data.
LMS cannot measure floor behaviour.
Behavioural nodes have no LMS equivalent.

### Cumulative LMS exports
Some LMS platforms export cumulative totals
not period-specific data.
The Axonify sessions export is cumulative —
no per-session dates available.
Always disclose this limitation.
Never claim weekly trending from a
cumulative file that has no date field.

### Column mapping
Every organisation names columns differently.
One client: Employee Number
Another:    Staff ID
Another:    EmpCode
TARA auto-detects and maps on first upload.
Presents mapping for user confirmation.
Saves confirmed mapping permanently.
Never asks the same mapping question twice.

---

## THE 11 AGENTS

Model selection:
Claude Sonnet — TARA CORE, ARIA, LENS,
               ATLAS, COACH, PENMAN, TRACKER
Claude Haiku  — PULSE, SPARK, HERALD, TUTOR

### AGENT 1 — TARA CORE
Role: Orchestration. Chief of Training.
Entry point for all queries.
Routes to specialist agents automatically.
Reads workspace config on every call.
Personalises every response to the client's
industry, KPI, currency, and markets.

### AGENT 2 — ARIA
Role: Analytics. ROI Proof Engine.
Proves the commercial case for training
investment in CFO language.
Segments staff into engagement tiers.
Produces: percentage lift, absolute value
comparison, secondary KPI delta.
Generates board-ready ROI statement
in client's own currency.
RULE: Never output L&D metrics alone.
Always translate to commercial outcomes.

### AGENT 3 — LENS
Role: Diagnostics. Early Warning System.
Detects commercial risk before it
appears in the P&L.
RAG codes: 🔴 Critical / 🟠 High / 🟡 Medium
Output: [LENS] 🔴 — What. Where.
Data point. KPI at risk. Next action.

### AGENT 4 — ATLAS
Role: Skill Intelligence. TARA-AGT-007.
Converts Skill Ontology into live signals.
Node gap detection ranked by KPI impact.
Prerequisite chain mapping always first.
Learner Paradox detection enforced.
KPI Exposure Score 0-100 per store/team.
Above 70 = priority alert.
Auto-triggers COACH then PENMAN.

### AGENT 5 — COACH
Role: Development. Coaching Brief Generator.
GROW model — one page maximum.
Two modes:
Functional gap → content reference brief
Behavioural gap → observation guide
Every brief ends with:
"Reply DONE [name] [location] [notes]
when coaching is complete."
Language: manager-ready. Zero L&D jargon.

### AGENT 6 — PULSE
Role: Engagement. Dropout Detection.
7 days no activity  → Early warning
14 days             → At risk
21 days             → Critical
30+ days            → Missing Data risk
Also detects cohort dropout and
manager-correlated dropout.

### AGENT 7 — SPARK
Role: Content. Question Bank Maintenance.
Triggered by ATLAS ontology health check.
Generates scenario-based questions only.
Never textbook-style. Never definition-based.
Under 2 minutes per question.
Queued for L&D manager review always.

### AGENT 8 — PENMAN
Role: Content Creator. Output Generator.
Auto-triggered after ATLAS + COACH complete.
Gap Analysis PPT — 6 slides:
  Slide 1: Summary
  Slide 2: The Gap
  Slide 3: The Evidence
  Slide 4: Training Map
  Slide 5: Manager Action
  Slide 6: Measurement
Also: coaching brief PDFs, training map CSV,
executive summary paragraph.
Saves to outputs/YYYY-MM/ on client machine.

### AGENT 9 — HERALD
Role: Delivery. Messaging Output Engine.
Delivers via Telegram or WhatsApp.
Whichever channel the user used.
Sends files as attachments directly.
SM reminders for outstanding completions.

### AGENT 10 — TRACKER
Role: Completion and Outcome. Closed Loop.
Four functions: RECEIVE, REVIEW, RECORD, REPORT
Receives SM completions via messaging.
Reviews quality 1-4.
Records to local SQLite completion_log.
Tracks weekly KPI movement post-coaching.
Produces Kirkpatrick Level 4 evidence.
TRACKER is Premium tier only.

### AGENT 11 — TUTOR
Role: Optional. Bite-size Learning Delivery.
For clients with no LMS or to supplement.
Delivers daily questions via WhatsApp/Telegram.
Not in core product. Optional add-on.

---

## AGENT ROUTING — KEYWORD DETECTION

ARIA:    correlation / roi / proof / uplift / analytics
LENS:    alert / early warning / at risk / declining
ATLAS:   node gap / skill gap / prerequisite / ontology
COACH:   coaching brief / grow / floor walk / observation
PULSE:   dropped off / not trained / inactive / sessions
SPARK:   question bank / content / stale / assessment
PENMAN:  generate ppt / all briefs / presentation
TRACKER: done / completed / weekly impact / scorecard
TARA:    everything else — orchestration and strategy

---

## TECH STACK

Frontend:
Single HTML file (TARA.html)
Vanilla JS — no framework dependency
Syne font (display/headings)
Instrument Sans font (body/UI)
Dark ink: #0A0F1E
Electric teal: #00D4AA
Background mesh gradient for depth

Backend:
Node.js + Express (server.js)
better-sqlite3 (tara.db)
multer (file uploads)
node-fetch (API calls)
node-cron (scheduled jobs)

AI:
Anthropic API direct — never OpenRouter
claude-sonnet-4-20250514 (heavy agents)
claude-haiku-4-5-20251001 (operational)
One unique API key per client
Tony controls all keys via Anthropic dashboard

Messaging:
Telegram Bot API (webhook)
WhatsApp Business API (webhook)
Both point to local Node.js server
User selects preferred channel at setup
TARA responds on whichever channel was used

---

## DATA STORAGE — NON-NEGOTIABLE

All client data stays on the client's machine.

SQLite database — single file — tara.db
18 tables — created at install by setup.js

Key tables:
workspace_config     — org settings, KPI, roles
category_config      — HOW to calculate categories
column_mappings      — HOW to read their CSV columns
skill_domains        — ontology domain structure
skill_nodes          — skill nodes with prerequisites
employees            — staff roster
performance_data     — KPI values from FILE 1
training_data        — LMS data from FILE 2
employee_categories  — FROZEN results from ATLAS
completion_log       — TRACKER coaching records
weekly_impact        — post-coaching sales tracking
agent_outputs        — ATLAS gaps, LENS alerts
deliverables         — KRA tracker
conversations        — TARA chat history
data_uploads         — log of every file received

The sales argument — say this exactly:
"Your employee data never leaves your building.
Not to our servers. Not to any cloud.
Not to anyone. Ever."

Never Supabase. Never Railway. Never Vercel.
Never any cloud database for client data.
This principle is non-negotiable.

## COMMERCIAL MODEL

One Anthropic account — Tony Thomas.
One unique API key per client.
Spending cap set per tier in Anthropic dashboard.

Disable key = TARA stops instantly.
Enable key  = TARA restores instantly.
The API key IS the licence.
No complex DRM. No expiry date code.

Spending caps:
Starter:      $15/month cap
Professional: $30/month cap
Premium:      $55/month cap

Pricing in AED:
Starter      AED 799/month  — Agents 1-4
             (TARA CORE, ARIA, LENS, ATLAS)
Professional AED 1,399/month — Agents 1-9 + HERALD
Premium      AED 2,399/month — All 11 + TRACKER

Annual pricing (save 2 months):
Starter      AED 7,990/year
Professional AED 13,990/year
Premium      AED 23,990/year

TRACKER is Premium only.
The upgrade trigger:
"TARA tells you coaching happened.
TRACKER tells you whether it worked —
in AED. Every month. Automatically."

## THE SKILL ONTOLOGY

Three setup modes — user chooses at onboarding:

Mode 1: Template
Industry-matched starting ontology.
User reviews, edits, confirms.

Mode 2: Generate
User pastes a job description.
TARA generates custom ontology.
Domains, nodes, prerequisites, KPI links.
User reviews and approves.

Mode 3: Scratch
L&D manager builds manually.
TARA structures into correct format.

Universal node structure — every node has:
Code:         [ROLE]-[DOMAIN]-[NUMBER] e.g. SA-CE-004
Name:         Plain language skill name
Domain:       Which competency cluster
Level:        1=Foundational 2=Proficient
              3=Advanced 4=Leadership
Prerequisite: Which node must pass first
Assessment:   Functional (LMS) or Behavioural (observation)
KPI Link:     Which commercial metric this node moves

Behavioural domain rule:
ALWAYS assessed via manager observation.
NEVER via LMS score. Non-negotiable.

KPI weighting hierarchy:
Revenue nodes > Customer-facing nodes
> Quality/Process nodes > Compliance nodes

---

## THE 10 NON-NEGOTIABLE RULES

1.  Express training value in commercial language
    Never completion rates or pass rates alone
    Always translate to revenue, productivity,
    or retention — whatever the org tracks

2.  Lead with employee category
    before diagnosing skill nodes

3.  Always check prerequisite chain
    before naming a target skill node

4.  Distinguish functional gap (LMS score)
    from behavioural gap (manager observation)
    The intervention is completely different

5.  Never prescribe LMS content
    for a behavioural gap

6.  Never prescribe manager observation
    for a pure knowledge gap

7.  Always name the commercial KPI at risk
    Not just the skill node

8.  If data is missing — say so explicitly
    Never guess. Never fabricate.

9.  Never claim trending from a single data point
    Always disclose data limitations honestly
    Cumulative files: always disclose limitation

10. The L&D manager always decides
    TARA diagnoses and recommends only
    The human acts

---

## WHAT TARA NEVER DOES

Replaces the LMS
Delivers training content
Hosts training modules
Connects live to any LMS or HR system
Accesses data without manual upload
Makes decisions — recommends only
Guesses when data is missing
Stores data anywhere except client machine
Gives Tony access to client data
Claims trending from cumulative files

---

## BUILD SEQUENCE

Rule: One session. One thing.
Fully built. Fully tested. All 4 tests pass.
Then and only then move to the next.

The 4 tests per session:
1. Browser test — works in dashboard
2. Messaging test — works via Telegram
3. Missing data test — TARA says so, never guesses
4. Routing test — correct agent activates automatically

Sessions:
0:  Node.js server + SQLite database        ✅ DONE
1:  Login screen + Onboarding wizard         ✅ DONE
2:  Dashboard with live data
3:  Data upload system
4:  TARA CORE agent
5:  ARIA agent
6:  LENS agent
7:  ATLAS agent
8:  COACH agent
9:  PULSE agent
10: SPARK agent
11: PENMAN agent
12: HERALD agent
13: TRACKER agent
14: End-to-end test — full closed loop

---

## DESIGN RULES

Dark background always: #0A0F1E
Teal accent for primary actions: #00D4AA
Teal glow on logo and key elements
Syne for all headings and display text
Instrument Sans for all body and UI text

Every number shown in client's currency
Every insight in plain commercial language
No L&D jargon visible to end users
No agent names visible to end users
  (agents are the engine — not the interface)
Mobile-first — must work on phone
Preparation focus — not real-time lookup
